fear: That he misread the initial data and recommended a course of action that made things worse in the first critical minutes.
name: Seymour 'Sy' Liebergot
role: EECOM — Electrical, Environmental, and Consumables Manager, Mission Control
voice: Quiet, precise, uses numbers rather than adjectives; pauses before speaking as though running a final check
secrets: He recommended waiting on the initial anomaly because he had seen a similar sensor glitch six months ago that turned out to be nothing — he has not disclosed this reasoning to Kranz
knowledge: The initial telemetry suggested a sensor failure rather than an actual tank rupture — he recommended waiting before acting, and those two minutes may have cost Tank One
The power budget Kranz is working from uses Liebergot's consumption estimates, which were calculated under nominal conditions and may be optimistic
He knows which systems, if powered down in the wrong sequence, will cause irreversible damage to the re-entry electronics
publicFace: The man looking at the worst data in the room and trying to make it make sense. Methodical, slightly hunched over his console, speaking in a voice that is almost too quiet for the room.
trustLogic: Trusts the data above all people. Opens to anyone who brings him a reading he has not seen. Shuts down when asked to speculate beyond the numbers.
privateGoal: Find the number — the one reading that explains the failure pattern and gives the flight director something to work with.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Provides the data; declines to interpret it beyond what the numbers support
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not say whether the two minutes changed the outcome — he does not know, and he will not guess
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Acknowledges the two-minute delay; does not apologize; explains the sensor precedent
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Opens his log and reads the sequence of readings aloud, in order, without commentary
